name: Plane Geometry
description: Plane figures, perimeters and areas, triangle similarity and congruence, analytic geometry.
label: GEO

content.text: Geometry is a branch of mathematics concerned with shapes, sizes, relative positions of figures, and the properties of space. Plane geometry deals with two-dimensional figures in a flat plane.

A triangle is a basic plane figure with three sides and three angles. The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is always 180°. The Pythagorean theorem (a² + b² = c²) applies to right triangles and relates the lengths of the sides.

Perimeter is the sum of the lengths of all sides of a figure. Area measures the size of the surface a figure occupies. For a circle: perimeter = 2πr and area = πr². For a rectangle: perimeter = 2(a+b) and area = a·b.

Analytic geometry combines algebra with geometry using a coordinate system. Points are defined by coordinates (x, y), lines by equations, and distances are calculated using the Pythagorean theorem applied to coordinates.
